question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data was officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026, showing the Consumer Price Index increased 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis in March. Since 0.3% is significantly less than the 0.9% threshold specified in the market question, this market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty. The current market price of 31% appears to reflect outdated information or confusion about the already-released data.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing March CPI increase of 0.3%
Market threshold requires more than 0.9% increase
Data release occurred on scheduled date (April 10, 2026)
risks: Potential data revisions (though historically rare for such large changes)
Misinterpretation of seasonal vs non-seasonal adjustments
Market may not have fully processed the released data yet
